title: City buildings (access-controlled)
description: Building footprints for a metropolitan area, published in a Portolan catalog. The STAC discovery metadata (this document) is public and static; the data assets live in access-controlled object storage. Reading is a two-step flow: (1) sign in at the publisher's consumer IdP (`publisher_oidc` scheme) to obtain an identity token; (2) exchange that token (`credential_exchange` scheme, OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange) for short-lived, prefix-scoped, read-only storage credentials; then read the assets directly from object storage.

publisher_oidc.description: Step 1 — identity. Sign in at the publisher's consumer IdP (any OIDC client flow, e.g. Authorization Code + PKCE for browsers). Yields an OIDC identity token.

credential_exchange.description: Step 2 — credentials (OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange, RFC 8693). POST the identity token obtained via the `publisher_oidc` scheme as `subject_token` to the `tokenUrl`. Returns short-lived, prefix-scoped, read-only storage credentials (`access_token` + `issued_token_type` + `expires_in`) for direct reads of this collection's assets — or 403 if the publisher has not granted this identity access. Credentials cover all assets of the collection (one credential, many objects: parquet + Iceberg metadata + manifests).

links.title: Data license and terms of access

iceberg-metadata.title: Apache Iceberg table metadata (access-controlled; readable with the same exchanged credentials)
